title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a world-renowned Indian dish featuring tender chicken pieces swimming in a rich, creamy, and mildly spiced tomato gravy. Its signature luscious texture and vibrant flavor come from a generous blend of butter, fresh cream, and aromatic spices, making it a truly unforgettable culinary experience. This classic recipe promises an authentic taste of India, perfect for a comforting family meal or a special occasion.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 mins, then ground to a paste

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with plain yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another 2 tbsp of butter. Once melted, add the chopped onion and saute until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Add 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using), and cook for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato and Spice Mixture
instructions.text: Pour in the tomato puree. Add the remaining 1/2 tsp red chili powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and salt to taste. Stir well and bring to a gentle simmer. Cover and cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil starts to separate from the sides.

instructions.title: Blend and Finish the Gravy
instructions.text: Stir in the cashew paste and sugar. Cook for 2 minutes. For a smoother gravy, carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. Return the blended gravy to the pan. Stir in the crushed Kasoori Methi.

instructions.title: Combine and Serve
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the gravy. Stir gently to coat. Let it simmer for 3-5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld. Finally, stir in the heavy cream and the last 1 tbsp of butter. Heat through gently without boiling. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander and serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.

tips: For the most tender chicken, marinate overnight. The yogurt helps to break down the fibers, resulting in incredibly succulent pieces.

Ensure you blend the gravy until perfectly smooth. This is key to achieving the signature velvety texture of authentic Murgh Makhani. A fine-mesh sieve can be used after blending for extra smoothness.

Kasoori Methi (dried fenugreek leaves) is a crucial ingredient for Butter Chicken's unique flavor. Toast it lightly in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing and adding it to enhance its aromatic notes.
article:
Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani: Your Ultimate Guide to India's Beloved Dish

If there's one dish that epitomizes the rich, diverse, and utterly irresistible flavors of Indian cuisine, it's undoubtedly Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani. This iconic creation has captivated palates worldwide with its luscious, creamy tomato-based sauce, tender chicken, and a delicate balance of sweet, tangy, and subtly spicy notes. More than just a meal, it's a culinary experience – a symphony of spices and textures that transports you straight to the heart of India with every bite.

Originating from Delhi in the mid-20th century, Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani in Hindi, meaning "chicken with butter") quickly rose to fame, becoming a staple in Indian restaurants globally and a beloved comfort food in countless homes. Its universal appeal lies in its approachable flavors, which are rich without being overly aggressive, making it a favorite even among those new to Indian food.

This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to master the art of making authentic Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani right in your own kitchen. From understanding its historical roots to step-by-step instructions, essential ingredients, and expert tips, prepare to embark on a flavorful journey that will impress your family and friends.

## What is Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani?

At its heart, Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani is a culinary masterpiece built around marinated chicken pieces, traditionally cooked in a tandoor (clay oven) but easily replicated at home, then simmered in a velvety, spiced tomato-based gravy. The "butter" in its name comes from the generous amounts of butter and cream used to enrich the sauce, giving it its signature smoothness and luxurious texture.

The sauce itself is a complex blend of aromatic spices, including garam masala, fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), ginger, garlic, and sometimes a hint of cardamom. Tomatoes form the backbone, providing a tangy sweetness, while cashews often contribute to the sauce's body and richness. A touch of sugar or honey can balance the acidity of the tomatoes, creating that characteristic sweet and savory profile. The chicken, usually boneless, skinless pieces of thigh or breast, is marinated in yogurt and spices before cooking, ensuring it remains incredibly tender and flavorful. The combination of these elements results in a dish that is both comforting and sophisticated, deeply satisfying, and endlessly delicious.

## Ingredients You Need

To create the perfect Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani, quality ingredients are key. Here's what you'll need:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp salt
*   1 tbsp lemon juice

For the Makhani Sauce:
*   2 tbsp unsalted butter (plus more for garnish)
*   1 tbsp oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder
*   1 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1 tsp coriander powder
*   1/2 tsp cumin powder
*   1 cup tomato puree (or crushed tomatoes)
*   1/4 cup cashews, soaked in warm water for 15 minutes
*   1/2 cup water
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (plus more for garnish)
*   1 tbsp kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1 tsp sugar or honey (to taste)
*   Salt to taste
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Mastering this Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani recipe is easier than you think. Follow these steps for a truly authentic experience:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with all marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring chicken is evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for best results.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, without overcrowding the pan. Cook in batches if necessary. Sear until lightly charred and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Remove cooked chicken and set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In the same pan, melt 2 tbsp butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
4.  **Add Aromatics and Spices:** Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Sauté for 30 seconds, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
5.  **Build the Sauce:** Pour in the tomato puree, water, and soaked cashews (drained). Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing flavors to meld and cashews to soften further.
6.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender (or use an immersion blender directly in the pot). Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silken texture, pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.
7.  **Finish the Makhani:** Return the pan to low heat. Stir in the heavy cream, kasoori methi, sugar/honey, and salt to taste. Simmer gently for 5 minutes, ensuring the sauce doesn't boil vigorously after adding cream.
8.  **Combine and Serve:** Add the cooked chicken pieces to the sauce. Stir to coat the chicken evenly and let it simmer for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the sauce.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

To achieve the authentic taste of Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani, consider these expert tips:

*   **Don't Skip Marination:** The longer you marinate the chicken, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination is highly recommended.
*   **Char the Chicken:** If you have a grill or a very hot pan, getting a slight char on the chicken before adding it to the sauce adds a beautiful smoky depth that mimics traditional tandoor cooking.
*   **Quality Tomatoes:** Use good quality tomato puree or ripe, fresh tomatoes. The tomatoes are the heart of the sauce, so their flavor is crucial.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** A truly great Butter Chicken sauce is incredibly smooth. Don't rush the blending process, and consider straining it for that restaurant-quality texture.
*   **Balance Flavors:** Taste your sauce frequently. Adjust salt, sugar/honey, and a squeeze of lemon juice (at the end) to achieve the perfect balance of sweet, tangy, and savory.
*   **Kasoori Methi is Key:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) are non-negotiable for authentic flavor. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their aroma.
*   **Butter at the End:** A final swirl of butter (or a dollop on top) just before serving adds extra richness and gloss.

## Serving Suggestions

No meal featuring Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani is complete without the perfect accompaniments. Here are some classic serving suggestions:

*   **Naan Bread:** Soft, fluffy naan is ideal for scooping up every last drop of the luscious sauce. Garlic naan or butter naan are particularly delicious choices.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice provides a neutral base that beautifully complements the rich flavors of the butter chicken.
*   **Jeera Rice:** Rice cooked with cumin seeds (jeera) adds another layer of aromatic spice.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt dip, often with cucumber or mint, helps to balance the richness of the butter chicken.
*   **Fresh Salad:** A simple side salad with thinly sliced onions, cucumbers, and tomatoes can add a refreshing crunch.
*   **Pickles and Chutneys:** A small dish of Indian pickles or mango chutney can offer a delightful contrast of flavors.

## Variations and Substitutions

While this recipe focuses on the traditional Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani, there are many ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Butter "Chicken":** Substitute chicken with paneer (Indian cheese), tofu, or even chickpeas. Marinate paneer or tofu similarly and pan-fry before adding to the sauce.
*   **Vegan Version:** Use plant-based butter, coconut cream instead of dairy cream, and almond or cashew milk. Omit yogurt from the marinade or use a plant-based alternative.
*   **Healthier Option:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or substitute some of the heavy cream with Greek yogurt (stirred in gently at the end to prevent curdling). Use olive oil instead of butter for sautéing.
*   **Spicier Kick:** Increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the sauce for more heat.
*   **Smoked Flavor:** Add a drop of liquid smoke to the marinade or sauce for an extra smoky depth, especially if you can't char the chicken well.
*   **Different Proteins:** This sauce works wonderfully with other proteins like shrimp, lamb, or even hard-boiled eggs. Adjust cooking times accordingly.

Embark on your culinary journey today and bring the magic of Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani to your table. This dish is more than just food; it's a celebration of flavor, comfort, and the incredible heritage of Indian cooking. Enjoy creating and savoring this timeless classic!
